The site provides career statistics for [Major League Baseball](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball "Major League Baseball") (MLB) players and teams as well as records, [MLB draft](/wiki/MLB_draft "MLB draft") history, and [sabermetrics](/wiki/Sabermetrics "Sabermetrics").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Baseball statistics database

Baseball Reference

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Type of site | [Baseball statistics](/wiki/Baseball_statistics "Baseball statistics") |
| Owner | [Sports Reference](/wiki/Sports_Reference "Sports Reference") |
| Created by | Sean Forman |
| URL | [www.baseball-reference.com](http://www.baseball-reference.com) |
| Launched | April 2000; 25 years ago (2000-04)[[1]](#cite_note-About_Sports_Reference-1) |
| Current status | Active |

**Baseball Reference** is a [baseball statistics](/wiki/Baseball_statistics "Baseball statistics") database maintained by [Sports Reference](/wiki/Sports_Reference "Sports Reference"). The site provides career statistics for [Major League Baseball](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball "Major League Baseball") (MLB) players and teams as well as records, [MLB draft](/wiki/MLB_draft "MLB draft") history, and [sabermetrics](/wiki/Sabermetrics "Sabermetrics").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Baseball_Reference&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Founder Sean Forman began developing the website while working on his Ph.D. dissertation in [applied math](/wiki/Applied_math "Applied math") and [computational science](/wiki/Computational_science "Computational science") at the [University of Iowa](/wiki/University_of_Iowa "University of Iowa"). While writing his dissertation, he had also been writing articles on and blogging about sabermetrics. Forman's database was originally built from the *[Total Baseball](/wiki/Total_Baseball "Total Baseball")* series of baseball [encyclopedias](/wiki/Encyclopedia "Encyclopedia").[[2]](#cite_note-rs-2)

The website went online in April 2000, after first being launched in February 2000 as part of the website for the *Big Bad Baseball Annual*. It was originally built as a web interface to the [Lahman Baseball Database](/wiki/Sean_Lahman "Sean Lahman"), though it now employs a variety of data sources.

In 2004, Forman founded [Sports Reference](/wiki/Sports_Reference "Sports Reference"). Sports Reference is a website that came out of the Baseball Reference website. The company was [incorporated](/wiki/Incorporation_(business) "Incorporation (business)") as Sports Reference, LLC in 2007.[[3]](#cite_note-BusinessweekOverview2-3) In 2006, Forman left his job as a math professor at [Saint Joseph's University](/wiki/Saint_Joseph%27s_University "Saint Joseph's University") to focus on Baseball Reference full-time.[[2]](#cite_note-rs-2)[[1]](#cite_note-About_Sports_Reference-1)[[4]](#cite_note-:0-4)

In February 2009, Fantasy Sports Ventures took a minority stake in Sports Reference, for a "low seven-figure sum".[[5]](#cite_note-FSV-5)

In late April 2021, the site changed a number of identifying names, "discontinuing the use of nicknames that are racially or ethnically influenced" and "names based upon a player's disability", such as [Chief Bender](/wiki/Chief_Bender "Chief Bender") and [Dummy Hoy](/wiki/Dummy_Hoy "Dummy Hoy"), who are now listed as Charles Bender and Billy Hoy, respectively.[[6]](#cite_note-:1-6)

## Features

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Baseball_Reference&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Features")]

### Statistics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Baseball_Reference&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Statistics")]

The site has season, career, and [minor league](/wiki/Minor_league_baseball "Minor league baseball") records (when available, back to [1888](/wiki/1888_in_baseball "1888 in baseball")) for everyone who has played Major League Baseball, year-by-year team pages, all final league standings, all postseason numbers, voting results for all historic awards such as the [Cy Young Award](/wiki/Cy_Young_Award "Cy Young Award") and [MVP](/wiki/Most_valuable_player "Most valuable player"), head-to-head batter vs. pitcher career totals, individual statistical leaders for each season and all-time, managers' career records, the full results of all MLB player drafts, [Negro leagues](/wiki/Negro_league "Negro league") statistics (Baseball Reference added Negro League statistics to its website in 2021), a baseball encyclopedia (the Bullpen),[[7]](#cite_note-main_page2-7) and box scores and game logs from every MLB game back to [1901](/wiki/1901_in_baseball "1901 in baseball"), among other features.[[6]](#cite_note-:1-6)

To compare ballplayers to one-another it offers "Black Ink" and "Gray Ink" tests, which tally a player's dominance and overall productivity against his peers. In 2012, it began to offer Jay Jaffe's [Jaffe Wins Above Replacement Score](/wiki/Jaffe_Wins_Above_Replacement_Score "Jaffe Wins Above Replacement Score") (JAWS) statistic for comparing players of different eras against each other by weighing their performance in the prime as well as their entire career.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Baseball Reference began including its version of [Wins Above Replacement](/wiki/Wins_above_replacement "Wins above replacement") (WAR) in 2010.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Its version determining a player's value differs slightly from other baseball statistics websites, including [FanGraphs](/wiki/FanGraphs "FanGraphs") and [Baseball Prospectus](/wiki/Baseball_Prospectus "Baseball Prospectus").[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### Bullpen

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Baseball_Reference&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Bullpen")]

Baseball Reference has its own baseball encyclopedia, a [wiki](/wiki/Wiki "Wiki") called "Baseball Reference Bullpen", which can be edited by anyone and is modeled after [Wikipedia](/wiki/Wikipedia "Wikipedia"). As of November 2025,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Baseball_Reference&action=edit) the Bullpen contains more than 123,000 articles.[[7]](#cite_note-main_page2-7)

### Easter eggs and other humor

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Baseball_Reference&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Easter eggs and other humor")]

There are a number of what the website calls "Frivolities."[[12]](#cite_note-12) Examples include:

* The Oracle of Baseball, which is no longer active on the website,[[13]](#cite_note-13) linked any two players by common teammates in the way the pop culture website "[Oracle of Bacon](/wiki/Six_Degrees_of_Kevin_Bacon "Six Degrees of Kevin Bacon")" does.
* A page devoted to [Keith Hernandez](/wiki/Keith_Hernandez "Keith Hernandez")'s mustache,[[4]](#cite_note-:0-4) which is the only "fictional" page on Baseball Reference.[[14]](#cite_note-14)
* The site made "[Tungsten Arm O'Doyle](/wiki/Wikipedia:April_Fools/April_Fools%27_Day_2023/Tungsten_Arm_O%27Doyle "Wikipedia:April Fools/April Fools' Day 2023/Tungsten Arm O'Doyle")", an internet meme associated with [Shohei Ohtani](/wiki/Shohei_Ohtani "Shohei Ohtani"), a redirect to Ohtani's page.[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* Although the standard player page notes a given player's favored batting and throwing arms, [Paul O'Neill](/wiki/Paul_O%27Neill_(baseball) "Paul O'Neill (baseball)")'s page adds his kicking leg and a link to a video of the 1989 game when he kicked the ball from the outfield to first base.[[16]](#cite_note-16) Likewise, the page for [Jim Abbott](/wiki/Jim_Abbott "Jim Abbott") (who was born without a right hand) adds "Fields: Left as well" in addition to him batting and throwing left handed.[[17]](#cite_note-17)
* The [2021 Seattle Mariners](/wiki/2021_Seattle_Mariners_season "2021 Seattle Mariners season") page lists the team's "fun differential" of +90, based on a comment from manager [Scott Servais](/wiki/Scott_Servais "Scott Servais") on the team's negative [run differential](/wiki/Run_differential "Run differential").[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20)
* [Oddibe McDowell](/wiki/Oddibe_McDowell "Oddibe McDowell")'s page features his monthly water bill payments from February 2011 to March 2012, which were public record for [Broward County, Florida](/wiki/Broward_County,_Florida "Broward County, Florida") and subsequently were featured in a recurring fashion on [Deadspin](/wiki/Deadspin "Deadspin").[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Baseball_Reference&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Sports Reference](/wiki/Sports_Reference "Sports Reference")
* [Pro Football Reference](/wiki/Pro_Football_Reference "Pro Football Reference")
